---
title: "plotnine-fluid"
---

# plotnine-fluid

`plotnine-fluid` is a small Quarto site for experimenting with the fluent
`plotnine` API. The notebooks here adapt examples from the plotnine gallery and
guide pages, with Polars used where it makes the data prep cleaner.

## Gallery Adaptations

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Anscombe's Quartet](notebooks/anscombes-quartet.qmd)

Recreates Anscombe's classic quartet with `plotnine`, `polars`, and
`scikit-learn`, including faceted regression lines and a polished final chart.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Please, I Want Some More Labels](notebooks/please-i-want-some-more-labels.qmd)

Shows how to add titles, subtitles, captions, and legend labels in a fluent
`plotnine` workflow.
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [A Bar Plot With 2 Variables](notebooks/bar-plot-with-2-variables.qmd)

Builds a grouped bar chart step by step, using Polars for the data and
layer-specific label mappings for the dodged text annotations.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Facets (Subplots)](notebooks/facets.qmd)

Demonstrates `facet_wrap()`, `facet_grid()`, formula syntax, and free scales in
a compact subplot-focused notebook.
:::
:::

## Guide Adaptations

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Geometric Objects](notebooks/geometric-objects.qmd)

Condenses the plotnine geoms guide into a notebook covering points, bars,
rectangles, lines, ribbons, distributions, position adjustments, and summaries.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Aesthetic Mappings](notebooks/aesthetic-mappings.qmd)

Focuses on global versus layer-local `aes()`, literal settings, grouping, and
expression-based mappings.
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Scales, Legends, and Guides](notebooks/scale-basics.qmd)

Covers manual scales, breaks, labels, limits, legend merging, guide placement,
and guide customization in the fluent API style.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Source Material

Most notebooks on this site are adapted from the official
[plotnine gallery](https://plotnine.org/gallery/) and
[plotnine guides](https://plotnine.org/guide/).
:::
:::
